message:
fix(state): always report counts_skipped=true on skip_db_open path

Follow-up to the gi4oy fix in f3dcce6d. state_meta_json_inner's
skip_db_open branch used `counts_skipped: !include_counts`,
which was correct only by accident for today's single caller
(state_meta_json_for_health pins include_counts=Some(false), so
!include_counts was true). The public surface
state_meta_json_full exposes both skip_db_open AND
include_counts as independent knobs, and a caller passing
skip_db_open=true together with include_counts=Some(true) — or
include_counts=None on a ≤256 MB DB where the size heuristic
returned true — would produce the contradictory envelope:

    { "messages": 0, "conversations": 0, "counts_skipped": false }

…because the snapshot never actually opened the DB to run
COUNT(*), so the counts fall through from StateDbSnapshot::default()
(zero), while the flag was reporting that counts HAD been computed.

Fix: hard-code `counts_skipped: true` in the skip_db_open arm. If
we skipped the open we never ran any count; the flag must report
that truthfully regardless of what the include_counts override
asked for. The callers that actually want real counts already
have to go through the probe_state_db path (skip_db_open=false)
by construction — no legitimate use case pairs "skip the DB open"
with "I still want valid counts".

A long doc comment above the arm records why this isn't just
stylistic: the previous shape was a data-integrity footgun for
anyone widening the callers of state_meta_json_full, and the
explicit `true` plus the comment makes the invariant visible at
the call site instead of hidden inside include_counts semantics.

Golden updates (basic_export.html.golden,
encrypted_export.html.golden) are incidental to this commit and
carry over the previously-staged copy-button helper changes from
adccde6c — landing together so the goldens stay synchronized
with the currently-emitted JS.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5649,10 +5649,22 @@ fn state_meta_json_inner(
     // open_skipped=true. The expensive FrankenStorage open is
     // elided; callers reading the JSON envelope branch on the new
     // flag if they need to know the open was assumed.
+    //
+    // counts_skipped MUST be true here regardless of `include_counts`
+    // — we never opened the DB so we never ran COUNT(*); the snapshot
+    // counts come from `..default()` (i.e. zero). Reporting
+    // counts_skipped=false alongside message_count=0 would be a lie.
+    // state_meta_json_for_health currently forces
+    // include_counts=Some(false) so `!include_counts` happened to
+    // yield true, but state_meta_json_full exposes both knobs and a
+    // future caller passing skip_db_open=true with
+    // include_counts=Some(true) (or None where the size heuristic
+    // returns true) would otherwise see `messages: 0,
+    // counts_skipped: false`.
     let db_snapshot = if skip_db_open && db_exists {
         StateDbSnapshot {
             opened: true,
-            counts_skipped: !include_counts,
+            counts_skipped: true,
             open_skipped: true,
             ..StateDbSnapshot::default()
         }
